message:
fix(ft-api): cross_entropy_loss_with_smoothing no longer severs autograd for label_smoothing > 0 [bd-ilwr]

cross_entropy_loss_with_smoothing composed correctly when
label_smoothing == 0 (delegates to nll_loss). But when
label_smoothing > 0, it extracted scalar values from nll and
uniform_loss via tensor_values, computed the blend in plain f64,
and rebuilt a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing
autograd for the entire label-smoothing training path. PyTorch's
torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy with label_smoothing IS
differentiable.

Fix: replace the scalar arithmetic with
    tensor_lerp(nll, uniform_loss, label_smoothing)
which evaluates the same (1 - s) * nll + s * uniform_loss formula
through autograd-aware primitives. tensor_lerp(start, end, w) is
the canonical FrankenTorch primitive for start * (1 - w) + end * w
— exactly the smoothed-CE definition.

Adds cross_entropy_with_smoothing_propagates_gradient_through_logits
regression test asserting:
  - gradient is finite throughout
  - at least one entry is meaningfully non-zero
  - gradient sums to 0 (since the log_softmax backward yields
    softmax - target_distribution, both summing to 1)

Closes frankentorch-ilwr. ft-api lib tests: 1151 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -106,6 +106,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-igu","title":"Add adversarial/fuzz test coverage for high-risk paths (spec Gate G4)","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §5 requires 'adversarial fixtures and fuzz/property suites for high-risk entry points' and §18 defines Gate G4 (adversarial + property tests) as release-blocking.

Current state: ft-serialize has 5 proptest properties. ft-core has none. ft-api, ft-autograd, ft-kernel-cpu have none. The spec's Security Threat Matrix (§16) defines 8 threat categories that need adversarial coverage.

Implementation plan:
1. Add proptest properties for ft-autograd:
   - Random computation graphs maintain gradient correctness (numerical gradient check)
   - Backward on disconnected subgraphs doesn't corrupt other gradients
   - In-place mutation version tracking prevents stale gradient reads
2. Add proptest properties for ft-kernel-cpu:
   - Broadcasting produces correct shapes for random input shapes
   - Reduction ops produce correct results for random input tensors (cross-check against naive impl)
   - Matmul associativity: (A*B)*C matches A*(B*C) within tolerance
3. Add proptest properties for ft-api:
   - Random tensor ops maintain shape consistency (output shape matches PyTorch rules)
   - save_state_dict -> load_state_dict round-trip preserves all values for random state dicts
4. Add adversarial fixture tests for ft-serialize:
   - Fuzzed JSON payloads don't cause panics (catch_unwind + verify Error returned)
   - Fuzzed binary payloads don't cause panics
   - Oversized shape dimensions don't cause OOM (our recent checked_mul fix)
5. Add adversarial tests for ft-nn:
   - NaN/Inf inputs to forward() produce NaN/Inf output (not panic)
   - Zero-sized batch inputs handled gracefully

Tests: The beads ARE the tests. Each property test should use proptest with 256+ cases.
Success criterion: cargo test --workspace --all-features passes with all new property tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:58.165045573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092380322Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092126241Z","close_reason":"Added 8 property-based tests: 4 in ft-autograd (add/mul gradient correctness, backward determinism, relu gradient indicator) + 4 in ft-kernel-cpu (neg self-inverse, exp/log round-trip, sum vs naive, softmax sums-to-one). All pass with proptest 256+ cases. Added proptest dev-dependency to ft-kernel-cpu.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-iha","title":"Add mixed precision support (torch.amp equivalent)","description":"torch.cuda.amp (Automatic Mixed Precision) is used in virtually every modern training pipeline. Even on CPU, F16/BF16 mixed precision can be useful for inference and for testing AMP workflows before moving to GPU.

Since FrankenTorch already supports F16 and BF16 dtypes in ft-core, the infrastructure exists — what's missing is the autocast and grad scaling machinery.

Implementation plan:
1. Add an AutocastContext to ft-api that tracks whether autocast is active and the target dtype
2. Add session.autocast_enter(dtype) / session.autocast_exit() (matching no_grad pattern)
3. Add session.with_autocast(dtype, closure) panic-safe wrapper
4. When autocast is active: matmul, linear, conv operations auto-downcast inputs to the target dtype (F16/BF16), then upcast output back to F32
5. Add a GradScaler struct in ft-optim that:
   a. Scales loss by a factor before backward (prevents F16 underflow)
   b. Unscales gradients before optimizer step
   c. Checks for inf/nan in gradients, skipping the update if found
   d. Adjusts scale factor dynamically based on overflow frequency

Key: The autocast policy (which ops to cast) should match PyTorch's known-safe list — matmul, linear, conv are safe; softmax, layernorm, loss functions should stay in F32.

Tests:
- Unit test: autocast context enters/exits correctly, respects nesting
- Unit test: matmul inside autocast uses F16, outside uses F32
- Unit test: GradScaler scales loss and unscales gradients
- Unit test: GradScaler skips update on inf gradients
- E2E test: train a linear model with autocast + GradScaler, verify convergence
- Panic-safety: with_autocast restores state on unwind","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:09.970716552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T23:42:34.450624484Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T23:42:34.450297625Z","close_reason":"Implemented mixed precision support: (1) ft-api: autocast_enter/exit/with_autocast/maybe_autocast with stack-based RAII context, panic-safe; (2) ft-optim: GradScaler with scale_loss/step/update + dynamic backoff/growth, overflow detection, skip-on-inf semantics; (3) ft-autograd: TensorBackwardReport::tensor_gradients_iter() and scaled_clone() helpers. 8 autocast tests + 7 GradScaler tests = 15 new tests. Note: F16/BF16 conversion paths gated until ft-autograd to_dtype supports them; F32/F64 down-casting works.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-iiv","title":"Reject impossible multinomial draws without replacement","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-30T02:30:49.283027420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-30T02:34:11.910610518Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T02:34:11.910332993Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-ilwr","title":"cross_entropy_loss_with_smoothing severs autograd when label_smoothing greater than 0","description":"cross_entropy_loss_with_smoothing at lib.rs:11272 composes correctly when label_smoothing == 0 (delegates to nll_loss). But when label_smoothing > 0, it extracts the scalar values from nll and uniform_loss via tensor_values(), computes the blend (1 - s) * nll + s * uniform in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf at line 11302. This silently severs autograd for the entire label-smoothing training path.

PyTorch's torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy with label_smoothing IS differentiable. Fix: replace the tensor_values + scalar combination with tensor_lerp(nll, uniform_loss, label_smoothing), which evaluates the blend through autograd-aware primitives. Same arithmetic but with the tape edges intact.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass scanning for tensor_values + tensor_variable(false) patterns.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Replaced the severed scalar-blend body with tensor_lerp(nll, uniform_loss, label_smoothing) which evaluates the same (1 - s) * nll + s * uniform arithmetic through autograd-aware primitives. tensor_lerp is the canonical FrankenTorch primitive for lerp(start, end, w) = start * (1 - w) + end * w. Added cross_entropy_with_smoothing_propagates_gradient_through_logits regression test asserting finite + non-zero + sum-to-0 gradient on logits. ft-api lib tests: 1151.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:13:52.249849573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:15:46.176875827Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:15:46.174567566Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-ioo","title":"Add CI pipeline with G1-G5 quality gates from spec","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §18 defines 5 CI gates that are release-blocking. NONE of them exist:

| Gate | Name | Blocking | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| G1 | format + lint | yes | lint report |
| G2 | unit + integration | yes | junit report |
| G3 | differential + gradient checks | yes | parity report + grad report |
| G4 | adversarial + property tests | yes | counterexample corpus |
| G5 | benchmark regression | yes | baseline delta report |

Implementation:
1. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with 5 jobs mapping to G1-G5
2. G1: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings
3. G2: cargo test --workspace --all-features (with junit output via cargo-nextest or similar)
4. G3: cargo test -p ft-conformance (differential fixtures), cargo test -p ft-autograd (gradient checks)
5. G4: cargo test --workspace --all-features (proptest runs as part of normal test suite)
6. G5: cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos (depends on frankentorch-vws being done first)

Considerations:
- GitHub Actions runners may be slow for full test suite; consider splitting into parallel jobs
- G5 (benchmark regression) needs baseline artifacts committed to repo for comparison
- Need to use nightly toolchain (rust-toolchain.toml already specifies this)

Tests:
- The CI pipeline IS the test — verify it by pushing a PR and checking all 5 gates pass
- Ensure the pipeline also runs on the 'main' branch (not just PRs)
- Add a badge to README.md showing CI status","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:38.761474655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T23:43:08.208191542Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T23:43:08.207936639Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: .github/workflows/phase2c_reliability_gates.yml has G1 (fmt/clippy) + G2 (unit/property) + G3 (differential) + G4 (e2e/forensics) + G5 (perf) + G6 (schema) + G7 (RaptorQ) + G8 (readiness). Exceeds spec's G1-G5 requirement. Uses rch offload when available, falls back to local. Has structured failure reporting with gate_id, replay commands, artifact refs, and reason codes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-ioo","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-3cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.351795619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankentorch-ioo","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-vws","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.900747037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankentorch-j2rj","title":"tensor_cross is severed-autograd: bilinear backward, easy fix","description":"tensor_cross at lib.rs:3354 extracts a_values + b_values, computes the cross product elementwise, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf. PyTorch's torch.cross is differentiable.

Cross product is bilinear:
   c = a × b   where c_0 = a_1 b_2 - a_2 b_1, c_1 = a_2 b_0 - a_0 b_2, c_2 = a_0 b_1 - a_1 b_0

Under chain rule with upstream grad_c:
   grad_a = b × grad_c
   grad_b = grad_c × a

Equivalent (and easier to implement) componentwise:
   grad_a_i = sum_j (∂c_j/∂a_i) * grad_c_j
The Jacobian is 3×3 and antisymmetric in a swap of (a, b).

Fix: wrap in tensor_apply_function with the bilinear backward. Inputs are [3]-vectors so cost is constant per call. Save a and b for backward.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass scanning for the values-then-non-grad-leaf pattern after closing tw0y.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_cross in tensor_apply_function with the bilinear backward grad_a = b × grad_c, grad_b = grad_c × a. Both inputs are saved for backward (each is a 3-vector). Added cross_propagates_gradient_through_a_and_b regression test (a = e_x, b = e_y, c = a × b = e_z; sum loss → grad_a = [1, 0, -1], grad_b = [0, 1, -1], asserts to 1e-12). ft-api lib tests: 1144.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:15:46.878708994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:17:35.882615282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T18:17:35.879838189Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
